Title: Overexpression of a Prefoldin β subunit gene reduces biomass recalcitrance in the bioenergy crop Populus

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitously expressed; high transcript abundance in stem/internode/node, immature leaf, and especially cambial region; decreased during xylem expansion and increased again during xylem maturation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Prefoldin β subunit; group II chaperonin; Prefoldin_2 motif (PF01920)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in Populus deltoides 'WV94'; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization in protoplasts; BiFC interaction assay; lignin compositional analysis by MBMS; saccharification assay; RNA-seq; GC-MS metabolomics
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to reduce biomass recalcitrance and improve lignocellulosic biofuel conversion; overexpression can be deployed without obvious height/diameter penalty in tested lines; may be combined with cell wall trait engineering focused on lignin composition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases lignin S/G ratio and enhances glucose and xylose release after pretreatment/hydrolysis, thereby reducing biomass recalcitrance; does not significantly change cellulose content or crystallinity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Lignin syringyl/guaiacyl composition; released glucose and xylose
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PdPFD2.2 overexpression in Populus increased lignin S/G ratio by about 7–8%. 2. Total released sugar after saccharification increased by 7.6% and 6.1% in two overexpression lines. 3. Cellulose content, hemicellulose content, and cellulose crystallinity/polymerization were not significantly changed. 4. Transcriptome and metabolome data showed altered phenylpropanoid, lignin, flavonoid, and salicylate-related metabolism. 5. PdPFD2.2 physically interacts with DELLA protein RGA1_8g, supporting a regulatory role in cell wall-related processes.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Phenylpropanoid polymer; cell wall carbohydrate products
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on biomass recalcitrance rather than nutritional quality. Metabolism-related outputs are lignin composition and saccharification-derived sugars. Phenotypic effects appeared to reach a dosage threshold because lines with different transcript levels showed similar phenotypes.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq 2500; accession NA); GC-MS metabolomics (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitously expressed; high in cambium, stem, immature leaf
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant reduction in plant height or basal stem diameter; one line showed increased above-ground biomass; broader effects on flavonoid and salicylate metabolism may cause secondary metabolic shifts
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth at 25°C, 16 h/8 h photoperiod; saccharification evaluated after hot water pretreatment and enzymatic hydrolysis
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Primarily outside nucleus when expressed alone; interaction with RGA1_8g associated with nuclear speckles
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Higher S/G ratio is associated with improved biomass deconstruction and biofuel feedstock performance; released glucose and xylose support biofuel production
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem secondary cell wall/woody biomass for lignin; released sugars measured from stem biomass hydrolysate

Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A DELLA protein that physically interacts with PFD2.2, but was not directly manipulated for metabolic phenotyping; included because the interaction is experimentally validated and linked to cell wall-related regulation. No evidence in this paper that it alone changes lignin or sugar release.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cell wall-related regulatory processes associated with lignin composition and biomass recalcitrance
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms an interaction pair with Pd/PtPFD2.2; BiFC signal observed as nuclear speckles
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression correlation analysis; subcellular localization; BiFC assay with PFD2.2 in Populus mesophyll protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Gibberellin/DELLA signaling inferred from DELLA identity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely guides PFD2.2 into nucleus and may influence downstream cell wall-related transcription indirectly; direct target genes not established
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly correlated with PtPFD2.2 across tissues and wood formation samples
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly associated with lignin composition affecting biomass processing
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GRAS family DELLA protein with nuclear localization signal
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated regulation; likely nuclear relocalization/coordination
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Phenylpropanoid polymer; cell wall carbohydrate products
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent interacting regulator connected to PFD2.2 action, but not proven as the decisive controller of lignin composition in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGA1_8g expression correlates with PtPFD2.2. 2. RGA1_8g is nuclear localized. 3. RGA1_8g, but not paralog RGA1_10g, physically interacts with PFD2.2 in nuclear speckles. 4. The interaction suggests a conserved DELLA–PFD regulatory module potentially affecting cell wall traits.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirect association with altered lignin S/G ratio and sugar release through interaction with PFD2.2; no standalone phenotypic validation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Pd/PtPFD2.2-associated downstream cell wall TF network; direct targets not determined
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential combinatorial target for understanding DELLA–PFD modules, but insufficient direct trait validation for immediate deployment

Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tested DELLA paralog that did not interact with PFD2.2 in BiFC; not a positive core causal regulator for the metabolic phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No interaction with PFD2.2 detected in BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression correlation analysis; subcellular localization; BiFC assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Correlated with PtPFD2.2 expression, but not validated as interacting partner
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GRAS family DELLA protein with nuclear localization signal
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGA1_10g is nuclear localized. 2. Despite expression correlation with PtPFD2.2, no physical interaction with PFD2.2 was detected. 3. Therefore it is not supported as the relevant DELLA partner in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No supported effect on target metabolic traits in this paper
